reviews.review_msg: Prof Reder is a very kind individual and tries to be there for her students. I found that there wasn't much course content, and the things we did speak about were very vague and theoretical. She tried to engage everyone, which made people want to participate less.

Deanna's a very charismatic prof, and the material she provides in her class is very valuable, but what she wants out of students can seem a bit scattered at times. Don't take the syllabus as gospel; things might change, and it can be confusing.
She was excellent! Very organized and invested in the course and her students. Went out of her way to make sure students understood assignments and expectations.
Awesome!

She is very confused as to whether she is teaching intro to FICTION or First Nation Studies. I have learned nothing about Fiction as a form, and a lot about First Nations. Super important, interesting topic- but I did not sign up for it. I really respect her as a prof, but the course material is not okay.

This class is not helpful if you are hoping to learn about anything to do with ENGLISH, this is an ABORIGINAL STUDIES course. I have not learned a single thing to do with English, She is a nice person who I respect greatly as a Prof, but I would not advise this course to anyone unless you are looking for an Aboriginal studies course.

The assignment outlines were vague and can lead to misinterpretation, make sure you talk to your TA for clarity! We had 3 books, a manga, and a handful of short stories to read. make sure you stay on top of your readings, she asks part. qus. in class. keep notes while reading, tab pages you like, needed for assignments.

Deanna is a great prof. She was open to extensions (for the whole class and for personal reasons). Deanna is great to set up a classroom with respect in regards to discussions of gender, sexuality and Indigenous studies. The assignments were fair and flexible. Would recommend!

Taken 4 classes w/ her. Best professor I have ever had. She's passionate about teaching, and helpful + supportive. Those who took ENGL 101 and are now complaining, it seems to me you are complaining primarily about the course content. Read the syllabus before you decide to take the class and don't take it if you aren't interested in the content.

Deanna is a very compassionate professor. Class was sometimes boring and she stutters a lot but she had cool activities throughout the semester which really help us learn. At first I thought the textbooks sucked, but both of them are amazing! I wouldn't mind taking another class with Deanna again. :)

Deanna is a wonderful professor. This is an ENGLISH course. People are simply reacting to the fact that the ENGLISH literature that we covered had first nations topics. The themes of the novels were first Nations themes, in any english course the professor takes a direction. If people were upset over it they should have dropped the course.
terrible professor and novels.

Lectures were very boring and dull but they are a must to attend because her lecture notes she posts online are useless (for example, she posts a picture on the notes but in lecture will talk on and on about it). Lectures are  useful for the short stories. Did not enjoy this as it was more first nations 101 than english 101.
Lectures were absolutely unbearable. Eng 101 isn't even english, its aboriginal studies. The books were all boring and some difficult to understand. TA's marking was unfair. Stay away, seriously.
makes you buy too many books. also we looked at a native comic book done in a japanese style this semester. not exactly english literature. talked too much about her race, nobody really cares.
Didnt really get much out of her lectures. Also, I didnt know shed make all the books native books. That wasn't really fair. Don't recommend this prof
Easy prof.. knows how to teach.. the slides suck, and you have to pay attention to what shes saying and take notes. Overall if you attend lectures and pay attention youll do good.

Easily the worst professor I've ever had out of my three years at various universities. The course pretends to be an intro to English fiction, but really is a Native studies course. More propaganda than English study in her poorly communicated lectures. Just miserable, truly miserable.

I'm sure if you took a first nations studies class with her it would be fine, but english is definitely not her priority. I think I learned more in grade 10 than I have this entire year. Her lectures are horribly boring and you read the absolute worst books...if you want english take it with another prof

Deanna is a bright, friendly, and very involved professor. She cares about her students and is extremely helpful. I was extremely shocked by the ratings of all the ENGL101 students who were so concerned about race. I loved this prof, taking another course with her just because she's great.

She means well and geniunely enjoys her subject, but seems to be a poor speaker & takes forever to make her points. If you're strong in English there is zero need to attend lectures; read books, do essays, and you're golden. She ran my tutorial as well, and was no more useful there. Emphasizes her own 'business' too much, as if her students aren't.

Deanna Reder is very helpful, i've asked her several questions and her responses were really helpful. And all of the people who are complaining, why does it matter if you're learning about natives? It's an english class, doesnt matter what you read or learn it's about improving your writing skills.

Deanna is amazing. The people who have given her poor ratings have been first years upset their English class featured exclusively Aboriginal lit. If you are at all interested in First Nations studies, Deanna is the best prof ever. She is incredibly clever and creates great class discussions and atmosphere. I really recommend FNST 201 with her.

She was very repetitive. As an introduction to fiction course, we talked only about Natives. The novels weren't very interesting and the TA's marked very difficult and didn't ever explain exactly what they wanted. The Prof says umm every other word and shows the same ppt slides in multiple lectures.
Overall a pretty excellent prof. Explains stories well, gives you more to think about in the stories and extremely helpful when you need further explanation.
TAs marking is unfair, novels are ridiculously hard to understand since it is about native stories
worst class i have ever taken. the TAs markings are soooo harsh. Novels used in this course is so boring that i fell asleep numerous times while reading

She is amazing. don't listen to first years who are judging based on the race of a literature. Deanna is incredibly helpful and caring and is always open to discussing your opinion. She encourages discussion and she works to open the minds of a eurowestern institution. Anyone who can not see the need for the types of classes she offers is blind.

The people here who have given Deanna a poor rating must be "out-to-lunch"!  She is truly one of the best, most thorough, and easily approachable profs at SFU.  Deanna cares about her students, and, more importantly, her subject--that's rare.  She is passionate and a joy to learn from.  Take her class.

extremely bad prof..dont bother taking her class. like other student said, it is a native course, not english. she talks too much and is really boring, people just leave her class halfway because no one can stand her.

I signed up for an intro to fiction english course. turns out since the prof is part of the first nations department all we're freakin learning is NATIVE BOOKS. 5 native books, its okay if u choose 1 or 2 but wow FIVE!! in lecture we learn about what kind of fruits natives eat and native reserves, NOT ENGLISH!boring not recommended,she cant speak.
she is not helpful, class super boring. students only attend because of iclicker. talks nonsense. just dont talk class with her..BORING! says UM a million times.
really great teacher lots of enthusiasm
Wow! Can't believe this professor has a good rating. By far her class was the worst I have taken at SFU. Her ability to communicate is severely limited by compulsion to misunderstand people.

The best prof I've had so far. Very understanding, very enthusiastic about the material, and very helpful with her studetns. I fully enjoyed this course and would definitely take another class with her!
Deanna is a great professor. She's very enthusiastic about the material and really likes students to get hands-on experience with the texts. Also, very flexible with due dates. Highly recommend!

Deanna is the best professor you can ever have. She is incredibly helpful and really cares. She is willing to meet you, helps a lot with papers and is really understanding. I took this class because there was nothing else for me to take and now will use all my electives just to take classes with her.
